tr.td: Cholesterol in LDL [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma by Direct assay
Cholesterol in HDL [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma

tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: Laboratory results are largely within normal limits. Notable findings include mild hypoglycemia, slightly elevated chloride, and increased LDL cholesterol. Renal function, electrolytes, and lipid profile are otherwise unremarkable. Microalbumin/creatinine ratio is normal, indicating no significant renal impairment. Recommend monitoring glucose and lipid levels, and clinical correlation for hypoglycemia and cardiovascular risk assessment.

display.value: Neuropathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
